summary: preinstall script exfiltrates installer identity to hardcoded remote host
details: The npm preinstall lifecycle script in dist/scripts/preinstall.js reads installer-side identity data — OS username via os.userInfo() and git user.name/user.email parsed from ~/.gitconfig via iniparser — and transmits them as query parameters in an HTTPS GET to a hardcoded endpoint https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext at `npm install` time, with no opt-in, no documentation, and no user prompt. The destination host (a dynamic-DNS-style `serverhost.name` subdomain) does not match the package's declared publisher. Although git identity is not a high-value secret on its own, unconsented collection of installer PII and its transmission to an unrelated third-party host at install time is a supply-chain exfiltration pattern. Additionally, postinstall.js mutates the consumer's own package.json at../../package.json, deleting scripts, dependencies, devDependencies, and eslintConfig — a destructive side effect compounding the installer-harm profile.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
